steps.title: Check current metal3-io deployment
steps.description:
Verify your metal3-io version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
helm list -n metal3-io 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working metal3-io installation.

steps.title: Review metal3-io configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metal3-io configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl get configmap -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metal3-io
```
With https://github.com/metal3-io/ironic-image/pull/452 in place for some time, Ironic has reasonable defaults for these values when paired with the default IPA-downloader. Not requiring them makes BMO installation simpler.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Stop requiring DEPLOY_KERNEL/DEPLOY_RAMDISK
steps.description:
This PR allows one to work with BareMetalHosts of different architectures while using kernel+ramdisk for preprovisioning.

It does this by adding new environment variables to configure the different URLs per target CPU architecture:
 - `DEPLOY_KERNEL_URL_<ARCH>`
 - `DEPLOY_RAMDISK_URL_<ARCH>`
 - `DEPLOY_ISO_URL_<ARCH>`
 - `DEPLOY_BOOTLOADER_URL_<ARCH>`

Non suffixed variables are used as defaults, if there is no architecture specific image(s) defined for the BMH CPU architecture.

It also adds

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl get events -n metal3-io --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Stop requiring DEPLOY_KERNEL/DEPLOY_RAMDISK" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR allows one to work with BareMetalHosts of different architectures while using kernel+ramdisk for preprovisioning.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metal3-io installed or the issue environment reproducible.
